keyPoints: A state in a game where no player can improve their payoff by unilaterally changing their strategy, assuming all other players keep their strategies unchanged.
Introduced by John Nash in 1950, this equilibrium is a fundamental concept in game theory.
Characterized by the concept of 'rationality' where players make decisions based on their own self-interest.
Key to understanding the behavior of players in a game, helping to predict the outcome of various strategies.
Named after John Nash, who was awarded the Nobel Prize in Economics in 1994 for his work on the topic.
